retrieved_docs.title: Hordeolum (Stye) -- Treatment / Management -- Severe or Persistent Styes
retrieved_docs.content: Treatment for severe or persistent hordeola includes conservative measures, medical therapy, and surgical intervention. Below is a structured approach following both national and international guidelines.

retrieved_docs.title: Brachymetatarsia -- Treatment / Management -- Conservative Treatments
retrieved_docs.content: Prevention of secondary issues: Conservative treatment targets the primary symptoms of brachymetatarsia to prevent or minimize secondary complications, such as calluses, corns, or other pressure-related problems.

retrieved_docs.content: The treatment aims to reduce the pain and discomfort resulting from corns. [11] [12]

retrieved_docs.title: Blount Disease -- Treatment / Management -- Guided Growth
retrieved_docs.content: The child should have at least 4 years of growth for this procedure to be successful. The Hueter-Volkmann principle, which posits that compression inhibits longitudinal growth, forms the basis of this treatment modality.

retrieved_docs.title: Corns -- Treatment / Management
retrieved_docs.content: Management begins with prevention. Patients should avoid ill-fitting shoes and mechanical trauma to the affected area.

retrieved_docs.title: Brachymetatarsia -- Treatment / Management -- Conservative Treatments
retrieved_docs.content: Conservative approaches may be sufficient for individuals who are asymptomatic or experiencing mild discomfort. These measures focus on alleviating symptoms and enhancing the patient's quality of life without requiring invasive procedures. The key objectives of conservative management are listed below. Pressure relief: Custom orthotics and padding can help distribute pressure more evenly across the foot, alleviating discomfort and preventing the exacerbation of symptoms. [15]

retrieved_docs.title: Infantile Botulism -- Treatment / Management
retrieved_docs.content: To determine the need for an advanced airway, continuous end-tidal carbon dioxide monitoring provides the most effective bedside assessment of respiratory depression. To alleviate respiratory distress, the Trendelenburg position at a 20° angle should be used, with a neck roll to stabilize the cervical spine and prevent sliding. If the gag reflex is diminished, the risk of aspiration increases.

retrieved_docs.title: Brachymetatarsia -- Treatment / Management -- Conservative Treatments
retrieved_docs.content: Improved comfort: Modifying footwear, such as shoes with a wider toe box, can accommodate the shortened toe and reduce friction, improving comfort, especially in cases where finding properly fitting shoes may otherwise be challenging. [15] [6]

retrieved_docs.title: Ingrown Toenails -- Treatment / Management
retrieved_docs.content: Treatment methods for ingrown toenails range from medical measures to surgical interventions. Indications for the treatment depend mainly on the stage of the condition, prior treatment modalities in case of recurrence, and other factors, including allergies to local anesthetics, pregnancy, and bleeding disorders. Conservative measures are generally recommended in mild to moderate lesions (stages 1 and 2), whereas severe lesions causing disability require surgical methods (stage 3). [8] [9] [10] [12]

retrieved_docs.title: Corns -- Treatment / Management
retrieved_docs.content: Any biomechanical abnormalities and/or deformities should be managed with appropriate conservative treatment. This involves proper footwear, orthotics, and soft cushions (silicon sheet, sheepskin), which reduce friction and improve comfort.

retrieved_docs.title: Brachymetatarsia -- Treatment / Management
retrieved_docs.content: The management of brachymetatarsia is tailored to the severity of the condition and the patient's symptoms.

retrieved_docs.title: Brachymetatarsia -- Treatment / Management -- Surgical Treatments
retrieved_docs.content: For patients experiencing more pronounced symptoms, such as significant functional impairment, persistent pain, or cosmetic concerns, surgical intervention may be necessary. Several surgical techniques have been developed to address the challenges associated with brachymetatarsia.
